zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret de Pype於1275年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾泰時，她的父親Thomas Pype15歲，母親Johanne Jarpenville15歲。她於1294年與Philip Somerville在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾維其諾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1349年8月在英國英格蘭萊希斯特斯爾巴羅 武朋 素亞享年74歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret de Pype于1275年出生在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾泰时，她的父亲Thomas Pype15岁，母亲Johanne Jarpenville15岁。她于1294年与Philip Somerville在英国英格兰斯塔福爾維其諾结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1349年8月在英国英格兰萊希斯特斯爾巴羅 武朋 素亞享年74岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pype
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German, Dutch, English (mainly northern), and French: nickname from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch pape ‘priest, cleric’, Old French pape ‘pope’, possibly for a person thought dignified or magnificent, or for someone with a severe or pompous manner, or perhaps a pageant name for someone who had played the part of a pope or priest. Compare Papa and Pope .

English (mainly northern), North German, and Dutch: from an ancient Germanic personal name Pap(p)o of uncertain origin, which occurs as the first element of the placename Papworth (Cambridgeshire) and Papendrecht (South Holland).

English (mainly northern): nickname from Middle English pap(e), pappe ‘gruel, porridge, baby food’ (Old French papa) related to pappe ‘breast’ (from Latin papilla), perhaps for someone who lacked the teeth for more substantial food.

variants.name: Paape
meanings: North German and Dutch: variant of   Pape  .

variants.name: Pappe
meanings: English: variant of   Pape  .

variants.name: Paap
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Manfred, Otto, Wolf.
North German and Dutch: variant of   Pape  .

variants.name: Papa
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Salvatore, Mario, Rosario, Domenic, Alfonso, Eduardo, Antonio, Basilio, Carmelo, Emidio, Ettore, Gino, Marco, Pasquale, Pellegrino, Pietro, Ricardo, Rodolfo.  Spanish Salvador, Joselito, Nicanor, Rolando.

Italian: nickname from papa ‘father, cleric, pope’. In southern Italy it is generally a nickname for someone thought to resemble a priest or, in some cases, for the illegitimate child of a priest, but in the North it is more often a nickname meaning ‘pope’, denoting a vain or pompous man. In some parts of Calabria the term is also signifies ‘uncle’.
American shortened form (and Greek feminine form, denoting ‘the daughter, or wife of’) of Greek Papas ‘priest’ and of patronymics beginning with Papa-, for example Papageorgiou .

variants.name: Papps
meanings: English (mainly Middlesex and Surrey): variant of Pape , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Paepke
meanings: North German: diminutive of   Pape  . See also   Papke  .

variants.name: Pfaff
meanings: South German: nickname from Middle High German pfaffe ‘cleric’ (see Pape ).

variants.name: Papen
meanings: North German: variant of Pape ‘priest, cleric’. It may be a shortened form of a compound name with Pape(n), for instance Papendieck (see Papendick ).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret de Pype was born in 1275, in Tysoe, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Pype, was 15 and her mother, Johanne Jarpenville, was 15. She married Philip Somerville in 1294, in Wichnor, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died in August 1349, in Barrow upon Soar, Leicestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 74.

ko.Crossingtonshire, Barrow-upon-Soar, Leic: 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 배로 업 소르
ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Pype(이)가 1275년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 타이소에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Pype(은)는 15세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Johanne Jarpenville(은)는 15세였다. 그녀는 1294년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 위ch너에서 Philip Somerville(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1349년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 레이스스터실르 배로 업 소르에서 74세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret de Pype родилась в 1275 г. в местности тисо, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Pype было 15 лет, а матери, которую звали Johanne Jarpenville, 15. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Philip Somerville в 1294 г. в местности вичнор, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла август 1349 г. в местности барроу упон соар, лисестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 74 лет.
